query: As of July 2026, financial and bankruptcy status of these companies: Chegg Inc, America's Car-Mart Inc, JetBlue Airways, Peloton Inc, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric, Petco Health and Wellness. Which are at highest risk of bankruptcy filing in mid-2026? Any going concern warnings, debt maturities, or restructuring news?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings:

## Bankruptcy Risk Ranking (as of July 2026)

**1. America's Car-Mart (CRMT) — HIGHEST RISK / IMMINENT.** This is unambiguously the answer to your question. The subprime auto dealer has **explicitly begun laying groundwork for a potential Chapter 11 filing** (per Bloomberg/TTNews, June 2026). It is living deadline-to-deadline on lender forbearance with Silver Point Finance after covenant defaults (June 12 → June 19 → amended to ~early September 2026 with milestones) and is seeking a **~$500M rescue loan via Houlihan Lokey**. FY2026 Q3 net loss was −$76.7M; it closed 42 stores and approved executive retention awards (a classic pre-restructuring signal). Stock ~$3.48 vs. a 52-week high of $62.53.

**2. Chegg (CHGG) — HIGH.** Existential AI/ChatGPT disruption (Q1 2026 revenue −48% YoY). Sub-$1 stock (NYSE listing risk), formal "strategic alternatives" review, ~half the workforce cut. Mitigant: actively retiring its **0% convertible notes due 2026** at discounts, easing the near-term maturity wall.

**3. Faraday Future (FFAI) — HIGH but slow-burn.** Nearly no revenue, chronic losses, **Nasdaq delisting notice (March 2026)**, reverse split, and serial dilutive financings ($45M new notes). Survives raise-to-raise rather than facing a single maturity trigger.

**4. JetBlue (JBLU) — MODERATE/ELEVATED.** FY2025 net loss ~$602M; Q1 2026 loss −$319M. Analysts flag elevated risk (Raymond James downgrade; ~75% bankruptcy-by-2027 cited), but ample liquidity, a new ~$500M aircraft-backed facility, and the JetForward turnaround make a mid-2026 filing unlikely.

**5. Petco (WOOF) — LOW/MODERATE.** Returned to annual profitability, reduced leverage, positive comps — improving trajectory.

**6. Peloton (PTON) — LOW.** Strong turnaround: Q3 FY2026 net income +$26M, adj. EBITDA $126M, FCF $151M, net debt −70% YoY to $173M.

Full structured data, sources, and quantitative data points have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool. Note: news-tool (AskNews) credits were exhausted, so the news component relied on web search, which was sufficient for cross-validation.

key_facts: America's Car-Mart (CRMT) is the highest bankruptcy risk: it has begun laying groundwork for a potential Chapter 11 filing (Bloomberg/TTNews, June 2026) and is seeking ~$500M rescue capital via Houlihan Lokey.

CRMT is operating under successive short-term forbearance agreements with Silver Point Finance after covenant defaults; deadlines ran June 12 -> June 19 -> amended late June to ~early September 2026 with milestones.
CRMT FY2026 Q3 (ended Jan 31, 2026) net loss was -$76.7M (EPS -$9.25); credit-loss provision $105M; closed 42 stores. Stock ~$3.48 vs 52-week high $62.53.
Chegg (CHGG) Q1 2026 revenue fell ~48% YoY to $63.3M due to AI/ChatGPT disruption; running a formal strategic-alternatives review and cutting ~half of workforce.

Chegg stock trades below $1 (~$0.89), creating NYSE listing-compliance risk; it has been repurchasing its 0% convertible notes due 2026 at discounts (tranches of $20M, $56.6M, $116.6M), reducing the near-term debt maturity wall.

Faraday Future (FFAI) received a Nasdaq delisting notice (min bid price) on March 20, 2026; shareholders approved a reverse stock split; company raised $45M new debt financing. Revenue negligible (~$0.5M/quarter).
FFAI stock ~$0.21 near 52-week low $0.2035; survives via continuous dilutive financings rather than a single maturity cliff.

JetBlue (JBLU) FY2025 net loss ~$602M; Q1 2026 net loss -$319M (EPS -$0.86). Analysts flag elevated risk (Raymond James downgrade; ~75% bankruptcy-by-2027 cited) but company secured ~$500M aircraft-backed financing and is executing JetForward turnaround.
Petco (WOOF) returned to annual profitability in FY2025, reduced leverage; quarterly EBITDA ~$75-93M; Q1 FY2026 comps +0.7%. Low bankruptcy risk.
Peloton (PTON) Q3 FY2026 GAAP net income +$26.4M, adjusted EBITDA $126M (+41% YoY), free cash flow $151M, net debt down 70% YoY to ~$173M; raised FY2026 outlook. Lowest bankruptcy risk of the group.
